concepts.headline: Essential books for product builders.
concepts.subtext: A curated collection organized by jobs-to-be-done. Three books per category, all completed, all worth your time.
data_points: Strategy: how to think about markets and positioning
Execution: shipping products that actually work
User insight: understanding what people really want
Systems thinking: seeing the whole, not just the parts
Communication: writing and persuading with clarity
Classics only: books that have stood the test of time
concepts.layout_description: Left-anchored hero layout on 1200x630 canvas. Eyebrow label 'PRODUCT READING LIST' in DM Mono 11px uppercase at top-left, 64px from edges. Headline in Inter 600 48px below, left-aligned, tight letter-spacing -0.03em. Seven vertically stacked rows each with a square number marker (20x20px, #f0efe9 fill, #e5e5e5 border, 0px border-radius) in DM Mono and a short category label in Inter 500 16px. Rows span left 55% of canvas. Right 45% is reserved negative space with a sparse particle dot network (8-12 circles, 3-6px, opacity 0.08) and hairline 0.5px connectors at opacity 0.10. A 2px warm sand accent bar (#d7c8af) runs horizontally at top of content block, 180px wide, fading to transparent. 64px padding on all edges. FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-left in Inter 700 11px uppercase, #888.
concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Canvas surface #faf9f6. Headline #111. Subtext #555. Number markers #f0efe9 fill with #e5e5e5 border. Category text #333. Eyebrow label #888 DM Mono. Accent bar #d7c8af. Particle dots #6b7a7c at 0.08 opacity. FLYWHEEL mark #888.
concepts.reasoning: The post is explicitly a categorized list of books organized by jobs-to-be-done, making numbered_list_graphic the natural fit. The stacked row treatment with square number markers reflects Flywheel's square geometry signature, and the sparse right-side particle network maintains the required 60% negative space while adding editorial texture.

concepts.headline: Read books older than ten years.
concepts.subtext: They are the ones that have stood the test of time. A curated signal-only reading list for product builders.
concepts.layout_description: Centered statement layout on 1200x630 canvas with #faf9f6 warm off-white background. A faint FLYWHEEL watermark in Inter 700 140px uppercase sits centered behind content at opacity 0.03. Primary headline in Inter 600 54px centered, color #111, letter-spacing -0.04em, two lines balanced symmetrically. Below the headline, a 2px warm sand accent bar (#d7c8af) 80px wide centered, at opacity 0.6. Subtext in Inter 400 18px centered, color #555, max-width 520px. Generous 80px vertical gap between headline and subtext. FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-center in DM Mono 11px uppercase, #888, 64px from bottom edge. Entire composition breathes with 70px padding all sides. No decorative elements beyond the faint watermark and accent bar.
concepts.color_usage: Background #faf9f6. Headline #111. Subtext #555. Accent bar #d7c8af. FLYWHEEL watermark #000 at 0.03 opacity. Brand mark #888.
concepts.reasoning: The Marc Andreessen principle buried in the post is the sharpest single insight worth amplifying. A bold statement card centered on that idea gives it the editorial gravity it deserves, referencing the Flywheel centered-statement pattern with maximum negative space and minimum elements.

concepts.headline: High signal reading for product builders.
concepts.subtext: Three books per category, all completed. Organized by jobs-to-be-done so you know exactly what to read next.
concepts.layout_description: Left-anchored hero layout on 1200x630 canvas. Eyebrow label 'CURATED READING LIST' in DM Mono 11px uppercase, color #888, top-left at 64px padding. A 2px warm sand accent bar (#d7c8af) 120px wide sits below the eyebrow at opacity 0.7. Headline in Inter 600 46px left-aligned, #111, letter-spacing -0.03em, max-width 560px. Subtext in Inter 400 18px left-aligned, #555, max-width 480px, 28px below headline. Four category items rendered as square-bordered chips (0px border-radius, 1px #e5e5e5 border, #f0efe9 fill, DM Mono 12px, #444) arranged in a 2x2 grid, 36px below subtext. Right 40% of canvas is open negative space with a faint particle dot network (6 nodes, 4px circles, #6b7a7c at 0.07 opacity, hairline connectors at 0.08). FLYWHEEL wordmark bottom-left, DM Mono 11px uppercase, #888. 64px padding all edges.
concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Headline #111. Subtext #555. Eyebrow #888. Category chips #f0efe9 fill, #e5e5e5 border, #444 text. Accent bar #d7c8af. Particle dots #6b7a7c at 0.07. Brand mark #888.
concepts.reasoning: The key_takeaway_card suits a post that distills a curated methodology rather than a raw list. The square-bordered category chips reflect Flywheel's square geometry, and the sparse 2x2 grid communicates structure without cluttering the composition. Left-anchored with open right space satisfies the 60% negative space rule.

selection_reasoning: The numbered_list_graphic best reflects the actual content structure of the post, which is explicitly organized by categories with a deliberate signal-to-noise filtering methodology. It demonstrates Flywheel's editorial precision through the stacked row treatment, square number markers, and left-anchored layout with particle dot negative space on the right. The concept is immediately legible and actionable, communicating the curatorial rigor of the reading list without marketing excess.
